sanskrit: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैःसह । सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥
hindiTranslation: इस प्रकार पुरुषको और गुणोंके सहित प्रकृतिको जो मनुष्य अलगअलग जानता है, वह सब तरहका बर्ताव करता हुआ भी फिर जन्म नहीं लेता ॥
englishTranslation: Whoever thus knows the purusha and prakriti together with the qualities is not born again, even while living and acting in every way.
essence: Clear seeing frees you while life still continues.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who clearly sees the true self, the body-nature, and the qualities that move them is no longer caught in rebirth, even while living normally.
simpleInsight: There is relief in seeing that awareness can be free even while life keeps moving.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna explains the deepest split in human experience: the knower, the body-nature, and the qualities that shape behavior. This verse says that clear seeing breaks the cycle of repeated birth.
modernRelevance: You are in the middle of a painful conversation, but part of you is watching the fear, the anger, and the urge to defend. That inner clarity changes everything about how much the moment owns you.
translationLiteral: यः (who) / एवं (thus) / वेत्ति (knows) / पुरुषम् (the purusha) / प्रकृतिम् (prakriti) / च (and) / गुणैः सह (together with the qualities) / सर्वथा (in every way) / वर्तमानः अपि (even while acting) / न (not) / सः (that one) / भूयः (again) / अभिजायते (is born)
